message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: State Fare Bar + Kitchen (Rancho Mirage)
Cuisine/type signal: Modern California / New American
Why it surfaced in discovery: Signature restaurant at The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage; remastered in 2024 as part of multi-year resort refresh; led by Executive Chef Bruno Lopez; Palm Springs Life lists it among Rancho Mirage reservations worth booking
Discovery sources: https://www.ritzcarlton.com/en/hotels/pspps-the-ritz-carlton-rancho-mirage/dining/ | https://www.marriott.com/en-us/dining/restaurant-bar/pspps-the-ritz-carlton-rancho-mirage/6468086-state-fare-bar-kitchen.mi | https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/6-rancho-mirage-restaurants-worth-a-reservation/

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

# State Fare Bar & Kitchen

**Cuisine:** American

**Description:** The restaurant celebrates California's agricultural bounty with "a menu crafted from seasonal ingredients alongside offerings from local wineries and small-batch distilleries."

**Hours:**
- Monday-Thursday, Sunday: 7:00 AM–10:00 PM
- Friday-Saturday: 7:00 AM–11:00 PM

**Dress Code:** Casual

**Location:** The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage (68900 Frank Sinatra Drive, Rancho Mirage, California)

**Phone:** +1 760-321-8282

**Menus Available:** Breakfast, lunch, and dinner menus are accessible as PDF downloads from the hotel's website.

**Chef Information:** No specific chef is named on this page, though the property offers exclusive culinary experiences with Master Chef Bruno on select days.

**Schema.org Data:** The page does not appear to contain visible schema.org structured data in the provided content.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about State Fare at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage:

Remastered in 2024, State Fare Bar & Kitchen is a modern California dining destination that celebrates the vibrant and diverse flavors of the region. State Fare Bar & Kitchen features a wraparound terrace, indoor-outdoor bar, firepits, and a new house-made pasta menu led by chef Bruno Lopez.

Under the imaginative leadership of Executive Chef Bruno Lopez, every dish at State Fare is a masterpiece, meticulously crafted with an unwavering commitment to the highest standards of quality and sustainability. Bruno Lopez is a member of the elite Maîtres Cuisiniers de France, which admits only the world's top-ranked chefs to its 350-member ranks.

The menu is stylishly appointed and seasonally inspired, offering artisanal dishes and curated cocktails that highlight the best of Southern California's culinary fare. The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage unveils a multiyear refresh of rooms, lobby and dining spaces.

I've found comprehensive review information for State Fare Bar + Kitchen in Rancho Mirage from multiple platforms:

## Overview
State Fare Bar & Kitchen is located in Rancho Mirage, California and offers a creative and refined cocktail selection along with California-inspired cuisine drawing from the finest purveyors in the Coachella Valley, inland Southern California and the Pacific coast. The restaurant was remastered in 2024 as a modern California dining destination.

## Review Ratings
- The restaurant boasts an average rating of 4.2 stars
- OpenTable shows 1199 reviews with one describing an "Excellent experience such a beautiful spot and service and food are exceptional"

## Highlights from Reviews
**Service & Atmosphere:** The staff is highly praised for their attentive and knowledgeable service, and the restaurant offers a wonderful dining experience with a stunning view of the valley and provides both indoor and outdoor seating.

**Food Quality:** The menu features options including avocado fries, burgers, and appetizers, with many customers highlighting unique spins on classic dishes such as Herb Chicken Mole and scallops. Portions are generally generous for mains with hearty servings, especially for items like scallops and short rib entrées.

**Mixed Feedback:** While most reviews are positive, some noted that menus posted online were not updated, so items that drew them to visit were not available.

The restaurant is located at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage and operates as a casual dining establishment with both bar and full dining options.

content.content:
# State Fare Bar & Kitchen - Rancho Mirage

**Overview:**
State Fare Bar & Kitchen is ranked "#11 of 80 Restaurants in Rancho Mirage" with a 4.0 rating based on 258 reviews. Located at the Ritz-Carlton, it serves American cuisine with bar and pub options.

**Rating & Recognition:**
The restaurant holds a Travelers' Choice award, given to establishments that "consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor."

**Recent Review Themes:**
Recent feedback highlights mixed experiences. Positive reviews praise the vegan ramen and mushroom steak, with one diner noting the latter was "perhaps the best I have ever had." However, critical reviews cite inconsistent food quality and high prices. One reviewer stated the restaurant "tries for a refined vibe, but misses the mark in every way," while another complained about a "$700 dinner for 5" that proved disappointing.

**Key Details:**
- **Hours:** 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM (extended to 11:00 PM Friday-Saturday)
- **Price Range:** $$ - $$$
- **Features:** Full bar, outdoor seating, live music, reservations accepted
- **Dietary Options:** Vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free selections available

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse**
Located within The Ritz-Carlton, Rancho Mirage, The Edge crafts remarkable wagyu steaks and seafood paired with an international wine list unrivaled in California's Greater Palm Springs Valley.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Established in 1978 by renowned restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is celebrated as the pioneer of fine dining in the desert. A longtime Rancho Mirage favorite, it offers a refined, unhurried dining experience rooted in classic desert style.

**Willie's Modern Fare**
Restaurateur Willie Rhine seamlessly pairs five-star hospitality with the elegance of fine dining, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar**
One of the newer names on Rancho Mirage's dining scene, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar is a family-run spot featuring steaks and osso buco complemented by an array of seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

**Si Bon**
Si Bon is a culinary gem that offers a delightful fusion of French and Belgian cuisine with its charming and inviting atmosphere.

These restaurants offer a range of culinary experiences from steakhouse classics to modern California cuisine, all with upscale service and atmospheres perfect for special occasions.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent California cuisine options in Rancho Mirage for a date night:

## Top California Cuisine Recommendations

**Wally's Desert Turtle** - An official California dining destination offering a refreshed take on modern California cuisine, proudly supporting local ranches, farms and fisheries committed to sustainable practices. It features polished service, elegant California cuisine and signature soufflés. This longtime Rancho Mirage favorite offers a refined, unhurried dining experience rooted in classic desert style, inviting guests to settle in with a martini and good conversation.

**State Fare Bar & Kitchen** - A modern California dining destination that celebrates vibrant and diverse flavors of the region, featuring fresh ingredients with a seasonally inspired menu offering artisanal dishes and curated cocktails highlighting the best of Southern California's culinary fare.

**Willie's Modern Fare** - Offers classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list, with guests praising the warm, inviting lounge with cozy seating and dark-toned dining rooms that exude sophistication and comfort.

## Fine Dining Steakhouse Option

**The Edge Steakhouse** - Perched atop the Coachella Valley at The Ritz-Carlton, this luxurious and elegant dining destination offers an exceptional experience with fine selections of dry-aged American Wagyu cuts and signature cocktails.

All of these restaurants are well-suited for a romantic date night in the Coachella Valley area.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chef Bruno Lopez:

Chef Bruno Lopez is the executive chef at the Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, and despite his Latin-sounding last name, he is French. 

Lopez has been a member of the prestigious Association des Maitres Cuisiniers de France (French Master Chef) since 2008, with only 350 members worldwide. Before joining Hotel Bel-Air, Lopez expanded his Executive Chef resume with Raffles L'Ermitage Hotel in Beverly Hills, the Regent Beverly Wilshire, The Ritz-Carlton Toronto and Rancho Mirage.

Lopez is a member of the James Beard Foundation, Club Culinaire of Los Angeles, Les Toques Blanches International, Confrérie de la Chaîne des Rotisseurs and L'Académie Culinaire de France. Additionally, in 2004 and 2011, Lopez was recognized by the French government as l'Officier de L'Ordre du Mérite Agricole.

Based on the search results, here are the key findings about State Fare Bar & Kitchen in Rancho Mirage:

## Recognition and Awards

Time Out highlights Palm Springs' best restaurants, including State Fare Bar & Kitchen at The Ritz-Carlton Rancho Mirage, noting seasonal California fare, seafood linguine, and a refined setting with valley views. Additionally, GAYOT.com awards it a rating of 14 on a 20-point scale.

## Restaurant Details

State Fare Bar & Kitchen is rated 4.0 of 5 on Tripadvisor and ranked #13 of 68 restaurants in Rancho Mirage. Remastered in 2024, State Fare Bar & Kitchen is a modern California dining destination that celebrates vibrant and diverse flavors, featuring fresh ingredients with a seasonally inspired menu offering artisanal dishes and curated cocktails that highlight the best of Southern California's culinary fare.

The restaurant is led by Executive Chef Bruno Lopez, under whose imaginative leadership every dish is meticulously crafted with an unwavering commitment to quality and sustainability.

Web search results for query: "best restaurants Coachella Valley 2026 California cuisine"

Links: [{"title":"The 10 best restaurants near Coachella Valley Preserve (2026 ranked)","url":"https://evendo.com/locations/california/palm-springs/attraction/coachella-valley-preserve/best-restaurants"},{"title":"8 Places to Eat in the Coachella Valley With Scenic Outdoor Patios | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/8-places-to-eat-in-the-coachella-valley-with-scenic-outdoor-patios/"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella Valley - Restaurant Guides","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g10212293-Coachella_Valley_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Restaurants&find_loc=Coachella,+CA"},{"title":"The Best 187 Restaurants Near Coachella Valley History Museum | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/landmark/restaurants-near-coachella-valley-museum"},{"title":"THE 10 BEST Restaurants in Coachella (Updated April 2026)","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurants-g32226-Coachella_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"18 Best Restaurants in the Coachella Valley | AvantStay","url":"https://avantstay.com/blog/best-restaurants-coachella-valley/"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in the Coachella Valley for 2026, with several featuring California cuisine:

## California Cuisine Specialists

Cork Tree Restaurant in Palm Desert offers exquisite Californian cuisine with dishes that tell a story of flavor and freshness. Sweet Basil California Eatery in Palm Desert features vibrant California flavors where every dish tells a story. The Hideout Kitchen + Bar in Palm Desert features the best of California cuisine with fresh ingredients and exceptional flavors.

## Fine Dining & Upscale Options

Spencer's in Palm Springs is a classic restaurant with a lush garden patio featuring shade trees and twinkle lights, offering a California-inspired menu. Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list, and reservations are recommended due to its popularity.

## Additional Notable Restaurants

Oceana Restaurant in Palm Desert offers fine dining with fresh seafood and exquisite flavors. La Quinta Cliffhouse is renowned for its spectacular valley views and serves American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood. Jackalope Ranch in Indio features Southwestern and American cuisine with a rustic and lively atmosphere and beautiful outdoor seating.

content.text: Assessment submitted. Key takeaways: State Fare Bar + Kitchen is a resort-owned (Ritz-Carlton / Marriott) signature restaurant with credible chef pedigree (Bruno Lopez, Maitres Cuisiniers de France), strong post-2024-remaster press, and decent platform reviews (Tripadvisor 4.0/258, Travelers' Choice; Yelp ~285). It surfaces well on Rancho Mirage-specific queries but loses to independents on broader "Coachella Valley California cuisine" queries, and the canonical Ritz dining page lacks visible schema.org markup with PDF-only menus. Classified as anchor-reference (not convertible) — useful as a map landmark for the resort-bracket, not a lead.
